Validate no artificial data limits in workflow

Source: R/sanity_checks.R
mysterycall_check_no_limits.Rd

Checks for common anti-patterns that limit data processing. This function helps enforce the package policy of processing ALL available data without artificial limits.

Usage

mysterycall_check_no_limits(
  data,
  context = "dataset",
  min_expected = NULL,
  max_expected = NULL
)

Arguments

data

Input dataframe to check

context

Description of where data came from, used in warning messages

min_expected

Minimum number of rows expected (optional). If provided and actual rows are less, generates a warning.

max_expected

Maximum number of rows expected (optional). If provided and actual rows exceed this, generates a warning.

Value

invisible(TRUE) when all checks pass. When a check fails, emits a base::warning() describing the issue and still returns invisible(TRUE) so the caller can continue processing.

See also

mysterycall_check_no_data_loss(), mysterycall_check_api_response(), mysterycall_scan_for_limits() for related sanity-check utilities.

Other utilities: %>%(), mysterycall_assess_data_quality(), mysterycall_check_api_response(), mysterycall_check_data_completeness(), mysterycall_check_dependencies(), mysterycall_check_no_data_loss(), mysterycall_download_file(), mysterycall_estimate_resources(), mysterycall_export_with_backup(), mysterycall_preflight_check(), mysterycall_quality_tier(), mysterycall_resolve_path(), mysterycall_save_quality_table(), mysterycall_scan_for_limits(), mysterycall_standard_labels(), mysterycall_standard_palette()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # interactive()
data <- read_csv("providers.csv")
mysterycall_check_no_limits(data, "provider input")

npi_results <- mysterycall_search_and_process_npi(data)
mysterycall_check_no_limits(npi_results, "NPI search results", min_expected = 100)
}
